completion_promises.criterion_text: src/forge/adapters/nats/specialist_dispatch.py implements NatsSpecialistDispatchAdapter with subscribe_reply, unsubscribe_reply, publish_dispatch.
completion_promises.evidence: Module created at src/forge/adapters/nats/specialist_dispatch.py exports NatsSpecialistDispatchAdapter with all three async methods (subscribe_reply, unsubscribe_reply, publish_dispatch) plus _on_reply_received callback. Verified by TestPublicSurface::test_adapter_exposes_three_lifecycle_methods which checks each method exists and is a coroutine function.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Subjects use singular convention: agents.command.{agent_id} and agents.result.{agent_id}.{correlation_key}. Verify via regex in tests.
completion_promises.evidence: COMMAND_SUBJECT_TEMPLATE='agents.command.{agent_id}' and RESULT_SUBJECT_TEMPLATE='agents.result.{agent_id}.{correlation_key}' constants are exported and used by command_subject_for/result_subject_for static helpers. TestSubjectConvention::test_subscribe_reply_uses_singular_result_subject and test_publish_dispatch_uses_singular_command_subject verify with re.fullmatch using the spec regexes (agents\.result\.[a-z0-9-]+\.[0-9a-f]{32} and agents\.command\.[a-z0-9-]+).

completion_promises.criterion_text: Headers on the published dispatch include correlation_key, requesting_agent_id='forge', dispatched_at (ISO 8601 UTC).
completion_promises.evidence: publish_dispatch builds headers dict with all three keys: CORRELATION_KEY_HEADER=attempt.correlation_key, REQUESTING_AGENT_HEADER='forge', DISPATCHED_AT_HEADER=self._clock.now().isoformat(). TestDispatchHeaders has four tests verifying each header is present, correlation_key matches the attempt, requesting_agent_id is 'forge', and dispatched_at parses as ISO 8601 with UTC offset. Clock injection (default SystemClock) keeps the adapter compliant with the project's clock-hygiene rule.

completion_promises.criterion_text: subscribe_reply() returns ONLY after the underlying NATS subscription is fully active. Test by asserting that a reply published immediately after subscribe_reply returns is received.
completion_promises.evidence: subscribe_reply awaits self._nc.subscribe(...) which (per nats-py contract) returns after SUB is sent, then issues a belt-and-braces flush() so the SUB lands at the server before return. TestSubscribeBeforePublish::test_subscribe_reply_blocks_until_underlying_subscribe_returns uses a subscribe_gate Event to assert subscribe_reply does not return while subscribe is parked. test_reply_published_after_subscribe_return_is_received calls subscribe_reply then immediately delivers via the recorded subscription callback and asserts the reply is forwarded to the registry.

completion_promises.criterion_text: PubAck on the audit stream does NOT trigger registry.deliver_reply() — it is observed by the publisher only as the 'publish was sent' signal, not as the dispatch outcome.
completion_promises.evidence: publish_dispatch logs PubAck at DEBUG only — no call to registry.deliver_reply on the success path. TestPubAckNotSuccess::test_pubAck_is_not_routed_through_registry sets nats_client.publish_ack to a sentinel object and verifies recording_registry.delivered remains empty. test_pubAck_is_not_routed_with_real_registry repeats with a real CorrelationRegistry instance.

completion_promises.criterion_text: unsubscribe_reply() is idempotent (safe to call twice with the same correlation_key).
completion_promises.evidence: unsubscribe_reply uses self._subscriptions.pop(correlation_key, None) so a second call observes None and returns early. TestUnsubscribeIdempotency has four tests: invokes_underlying_unsubscribe_once, twice_is_no_op (verifies sub.unsubscribe_calls == 1 after multiple invocations), unsubscribe_unknown_key_is_no_op (never-subscribed key), swallows_transport_error (RuntimeError from sub.unsubscribe is caught and logged).

completion_promises.criterion_text: _on_reply_received() extracts the source agent ID from msg headers and forwards to registry.deliver_reply(). The adapter does NOT attempt to authenticate — that lives in the registry (TASK-SAD-003).
completion_promises.evidence: _on_reply_received reads SOURCE_AGENT_HEADER and CORRELATION_KEY_HEADER from msg.headers, decodes the JSON body, and forwards to self._registry.deliver_reply(correlation_key, source_agent_id, payload) verbatim — no source comparison, no authentication. TestOnReplyReceived::test_does_not_authenticate_locally explicitly verifies that an 'imposter-agent' source value is forwarded unchanged to the registry. Defensive drops (missing headers, malformed JSON, non-dict root, callback exceptions) are covered by six additional tests in the class.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Compatibility: existing tests/bdd/conftest.py:FakeNatsClient fixture is a drop-in test double — do NOT introduce a parallel test transport. Add fake-side recording for subscribe/unsubscribe events if needed.
completion_promises.evidence: The adapter's transport surface is restricted to subscribe(subject, cb=...), publish(subject, body, headers=...), and flush() — methods either present or trivially addable to the BDD FakeNatsClient. TestFakeNatsClientCompatibility::test_adapter_uses_only_subscribe_publish_flush builds a deliberately-minimal client exposing only those three methods and exercises the full adapter surface (subscribe_reply, publish_dispatch, unsubscribe_reply) against it. Per the task's Implementation Notes, the actual conftest extension (subscribe/unsubscribe recording) is deferred to TASK-SAD-011 — this task only proves the adapter does not require additional transport surface beyond what FakeNatsClient already provides.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors.
completion_promises.evidence: Code follows project style (Black-compatible, 88-char lines, type hints, snake_case/PascalCase, docstrings, f-strings, context managers). Module passes the project's clock-hygiene gate (tests/forge/test_contract_and_seam.py::TestClockHygiene) — the initial datetime.now(timezone.utc) call was refactored to inject a Clock dependency (defaulting to SystemClock) which is the project-canonical pattern used by DiscoveryCache. No other lint configuration (ruff, flake8, mypy) is committed at the repo root.
